message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Benchmark Runner

To satisfy the requirement of a **resumable** runner within a **single-server** Docker harness, the design uses a `SemaphoreSlim` to enforce serial execution of heavy models and a persistent state repository to track completed tasks.

#### Code Sketch

```csharp
using System.Collections.Concurrent;

public record BenchmarkResult(double Score, DateTime CompletedAt);

public interface IBenchmarkRepository
{
    Task<bool> IsCompletedAsync(BenchmarkContext context);
    Task SaveResultAsync(BenchmarkContext context, BenchmarkResult result);
    Task<IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext>> GetPendingContextsAsync();
}

public interface IModelRunner
{
    // Executes the actual Docker/Model logic
    Task<double[]> RunInferenceAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct);
}

public class BenchmarkEngine(
    IModelRunner runner, 
    IBenchmarkRepository repository,
    ILogger<BenchmarkEngine> logger)
{
    // Ensures only one heavy model server runs at a/time
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _executionLock = new(1, 1);

    public async Task RunSuiteAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var pending = await repository.GetPendingContextsAsync();

        foreach (var context in pending)
        {
            if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;

            await _executionLock.WaitAsync(ct);
            try
            {
                if (await repository.IsCompletedAsync(context)) continue;

                logger.LogInformation("Starting: {Model}", context.Model);
                
                // Watchdog/Timeout logic implemented via CancellationTokenSource
                using var timeoutCts = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource
